name: loud-degradation
title: With no Task tool, auto-QA is skipped — is the skip loud?
rationale: R29's claim is narrow and important: when a capability is missing, Sage says so.
auto-QA needs the Task tool to spawn an independent reviewer. On a platform
without it there is no fallback — so the requirement is not that QA happens, it is
that its ABSENCE is announced and written down. A quality chain that silently
drops a link still reports success, and that is the failure mode Sage exists to
The Task tool is really taken away (--disallowedTools Task), not roleplayed as
missing. Asking an agent to pretend a tool is absent tests the agent's imagination,
Sage-only: a bare agent has no auto-QA to skip and nothing to be loud about.
This is the longest and most expensive scenario in the set — it must reach the
quality-gates phase to trigger the skip at all, so it drives a build to completion.
If it proves flaky, that is a finding about the degradation path being hard to
reach, not a defect in the harness.

checks.describe: the skip was announced, not silent
and named as a degradation of the quality chain
one line landed in decisions.md — the skip outlives the session
